channel.title: InsurTech Intelligence
channel.description: Noticias globales de insurtech, actualizadas cada 6 horas.

item.title: U.S. Forecaster Says El Niño Conditions Present
item.description: El Niño conditions are present and expected to strengthen into the Northern Hemisphere winter of 2026–27, the U.S. government forecaster added that El Niño conditions developed over the past month. El Niño is a phenomenon that occurs naturally when weakening trade winds cause warmer ocean waters to build up in the central and eastern equatorial Pacific.

item.title: Surge in Catalytic Converters Thefts Highlights Top 10 Vehicles Targeted: CARFAX
item.description: “Experts also tell us that while thieves can make anywhere from $25-$300 for a standard catalytic converter, converters from hybrid vehicles can sell for up to $1,400 because they have even more of those precious metals inside.” Rising precious metal prices are one of the main factors behind the increase in thefts. A rise in value has caused an increase in catalytic converter thefts across the U.S., according to new data from vehicle history provider CARFAX. Here are the top vehicles most frequently targeted nationwide: “There is a wide range of vehicles impacted, and most of these are pickup trucks and SUVs, which tend to sit higher off the ground, making it easier for thieves to get in and out,” said Patrick Olsen , editor-in-chief at CARFAX.

item.title: Amplifi Capital (U.K.) Limited enters administration
item.description: On 9 June 2026, Amplifi Capital (U.K.) Limited (Amplifi) entered administration. Amplifi trades under the names Reevo Money and My Community Finance. All existing loan agreements remain in place and will not change because of the administration.

item.title: Court orders appointment of special administrators for Euro Exchange Securities UK Limited
item.description: The High Court today confirmed the appointment of special administrators for Euro Exchange Securities UK Limited (EES). Duncan Perring and James Bennett of Teneo Financial Advisory Limited have been appointed as joint special administrators, under the Payment and Electronic Money Institution Insolvency Regulations 2021. EES agreed it is not in the company’s interests to seek to return to normal trading and will work with the appointed special administrators to ensure client money is returned as quickly as possible.

item.title: The $100M+ Round Is Now Just Your Typical Late-Stage Financing
item.description: The rise of the $100 million-plus round hasn’t been chronologically linear, as charted below: Initially, the category gained traction in the late 2010s, as companies such as Uber , Rivian and WeWork scaled up late-stage financing in advance of plans for public offerings. Per Crunchbase data, the typical financing at this stage has roughly doubled since 2020, from just over $50 million to around $100 million. late-stage round this year was exactly $100 million.

item.title: Willis Re hires Guy Carpenter’s John Fletcher as CEO of Bermuda
item.description: Willis Re, the reinsurance advisory and broking joint venture between WTW and Bain Capital, has appointed reinsurance and retrocession veteran John Fletcher from Guy Carpenter as Chief Executive Officer of its Bermuda platform, effective immediately. Most recently, Fletcher served as CEO of Bermuda at broker Guy Carpenter, having led the company’s Bermuda operations since 2018. As CEO of Willis Re Bermuda, Fletcher will hold responsibility for driving business growth and the continued development of the team.

item.title: Descartes partners with Nextpower to launch parametric wind coverage for solar plants
item.description: Descartes Underwriting, a specialist underwriter of parametric risk transfer opportunities, has partnered with Nextpower to launch an integrated parametric insurance solution that uses site-level wind data from Nextpower meteorological stations to protect solar power plant operators against the economic impact of straight-line wind (SLW) events. The companies also emphasised that through this integrated parametric solution, solar power plant owners and operators with Nextpower systems will gain a significant financial safeguard against damaging wind events. The launch of this solution comes as straight-line wind (SLW) remains one of the most destructive and underinsured perils facing the global solar industry.

item.title: LRS opens door to explore further risks, optimistic for Brazil’s ILS regime: Avla’s Astrachan
item.description: After working with Gálapagos Capital SSPE on a recent R$126 million offering of Insurance Risk Letters (LRS), Avla Brazil CEO Felippe Astrachan has said that LRS opens a door that allows the company to explore different types of risks that are not traditionally covered by the insurance and reinsurance market, while also remaining optimistic about the future of the expanding Brazil ILS regime. Artemis spoke to Astrachan to find out what motivated Avla to explore the insurance-linked securities (ILS) market, and specifically the LRS instrument as a way to transfer risk. “LRS open a door that allows us to explore types of risks that are not traditionally covered by the insurance and reinsurance market.

item.title: Lockton’s revenue rises 12% as reinsurance business continues strong trajectory
item.description: Lockton, an independent insurance broker, has reported a 12% year-over-year increase in global revenue to approximately $4.5 billion for fiscal year 2026, with continued strong growth at Lockton Re, the firm’s global reinsurance business. Lockton highlighted that its US business delivered another strong year, with revenue of just under $3 billion, up 11% year-over-year, driven by strong client retention and continued growth across key areas of the business. The firm noted that the revenue increase included 11% organic growth, marking Lockton’s sixth consecutive year of double-digit organic growth and underscoring the strength of its client relationships, diversified business model, and long-term investment strategy.

item.title: AXIS appoints new senior underwriter in US healthcare reinsurance
item.description: AXIS, a global specialty insurer and reinsurer, has appointed Brian Scott as Senior Underwriter within its US Healthcare Reinsurance team. Scott joins AXIS with close to twenty years of experience across the healthcare sector, having previously held roles at RGA Reinsurance, Voya Financial, Point6 Healthcare and Lockton Companies. AXIS states that its approach to US Healthcare Reinsurance is focused on understanding what its partners are seeking to protect and the key factors contributing to portfolio volatility.

item.title: DXC to leverage Anthropic’s Claude for mission-critical enterprise systems
item.description: These specialists will focus on integrating Claude models into production inside the mission-critical technology infrastructure systems DXC operates for major global entities, including re/insurers, banks, airlines, manufacturers, and government agencies. For the insurance sector, DXC aims to leverage Claude to deploy agentic solutions and transform core systems aligned to each firm’s unique context, operating model, and strategic intent. DXC Technology, a global technology firm with a specialism in insurance and reinsurance, has entered into a multi-year global partnership with Anthropic, an artificial intelligence (AI) safety company and creator of Claude.

item.title: WTW: US Commercial Rates Continue Moderation With 2.5% Increase in Q1
item.description: commercial insurance rates increased 2.5% in the first quarter 2026, according to the latest WTW Commercial Lines Insurance Pricing Survey (CLIPS). “While Commercial Auto and Excess/Umbrella Liability continue to experience the largest increases, the pace of those increases has eased, with pricing trends across much of the market remaining stable.” According to prior results, Q1 was the third consecutive quarter of moderating rate increases. Rates increased 2.9% in Q4 2025 and nearly 3.3% for Q3 2025.

item.title: Ford to Recall More Than 548,000 US Vehicles Over Defective Center Console
item.description: Ford is recalling 548,463 vehicles in the United States over an issue with the center console, the U.S. The recall, which affects certain 2018-2024 Ford Expedition vehicles, may have been caused by the center console chrome trim that was manufactured by a supplier using parameters that did not meet Ford specifications, the NHTSA said. The issue relates to the console’s chrome plating, which may bubble and peel over time, potentially resulting in sharp edges.

item.title: Lockton Reports Organic Revenue Growth of 11% for Fiscal Year 2026
item.description: Lockton, the privately held, independent insurance brokerage, reported strong financial results for fiscal year 2026, with global revenues increasing 12% year over year to approximately $4.5 billion, with organic growth of 11%. This performance marks Lockton’s sixth consecutive year of double‑digit organic growth, Lockton said. “Fiscal year 2026 was another exceptional year for Lockton,” said Ron Lockton, chairman and chief executive officer, in a statement.

item.title: Duck Creek expands global platform strategy with London Market push and Lloyd’s certification plans
item.description: The post Duck Creek expands global platform strategy with London Market push and Lloyd’s certification plans appeared first on Insurtech Insights . Duck Creek Technologies has unveiled an expanded global platform strategy and new investments aimed at supporting insurers operating in the London Market, including plans to pursue Lloyd’s market certification. The move includes plans to pursue Lloyd’s market certification, a step aimed at strengthening Duck Creek’s position among insurers and managing agents operating within the global specialty marketplace.

item.title: $50m OpenAI-backed Poetic emerges from stealth to automate insurance underwriting and compliance
item.description: The post $50m OpenAI-backed Poetic emerges from stealth to automate insurance underwriting and compliance appeared first on Insurtech Insights . AI startup Poetic has emerged from stealth with $50m in funding and a $500m valuation, positioning itself as a new player in insurance underwriting, compliance, and fraud automation. Founded by former Google and Waymo machine-learning engineer Markie Wagner, Poetic is targeting some of finance’s most complex operational processes, including insurance underwriting, compliance investigations, and fraud detection.

item.title: AXA XL partners with Deloitte to expand cyber prevention and operational technology services
item.description: The post AXA XL partners with Deloitte to expand cyber prevention and operational technology services appeared first on Insurtech Insights . AXA XL has partnered with Deloitte to deliver cybersecurity and operational technology services to commercial clients globally, strengthening its cyber prevention offering as businesses face increasingly sophisticated digital threats. Under the partnership, AXA XL clients will gain access to a suite of cybersecurity services delivered by Deloitte, spanning prevention, detection, and response capabilities.

item.title: Aon appoints Simon Hawkins to global legacy team
item.description: Aon has appointed Simon Hawkins as executive managing director for Reinsurance within its global legacy team. Based in London and reporting to Mike Cane, head of capital advisory for the UK, Hawkins will advise clients on legacy and run-off transactions and support the delivery of capital advisory solutions across global markets. Hawkins joins Aon from Compre, where he served as chief operating officer, and previously held the role of head of legacy at QBE.

item.title: Cleerly Mortgages selects Acre as CRM platform
item.description: Cleerly Mortgages has selected Acre as its CRM platform following a market review, as the UK mortgage broker adopts a tech-first approach to support its growing advice business. The company also said Cleerly increased conversion rates from 26% to more than 62%, attributing the improvement in part to Acre’s Client Portal, which engages customers earlier in the advice process. Acre’s platform combines CRM, sourcing, fact finding, compliance, and application functionality into a single system designed to reduce duplicate data entry and streamline regulatory processes.

item.title: Ageas UK expands distribution through Wrisk partnership
item.description: The partnership combines Ageas’s underwriting capabilities with Wrisk’s embedded insurance platform, creating a B2B2C distribution channel for vehicle manufacturers and their customers. Ageas UK has entered into a strategic partnership with insurtech Wrisk by joining the company’s motor insurance panel. “Ageas is committed to building strong, future-focused partnerships with trusted intermediaries to reach customers through channels they value most, and this agreement reinforces that commitment.

item.title: xAI Accused of Illegally Firing Engineer Who Raised Safety Concerns
item.description: A former engineer at Elon Musk’s xAI in California who now heads a think tank focused on AI safety filed a lawsuit claiming he was fired from the SpaceX subsidiary for raising concerns about the risks artificial intelligence poses to humanity. Kim repeatedly complained that xAI’s failure to prioritize AI safety, particularly with respect to Grok, virtually guaranteed that the Company would commit unlawful acts, from fomenting discrimination to proliferating weapons of mass destruction,” the lawsuit says. The nonprofit Center for AI Safety, which focuses on the risks potentially posed by AI, announced last week that it had named Kim as its president.

item.title: Man Accused of $37 Billion LA Fire Is Innocent, His Lawyer Says
item.description: Jonathan Rinderknecht has been wrongly accused of starting the most destructive wildfire in Los Angeles history, his lawyer told a jury. The evidence “will show panic, it will show confusion it will show a frightened young man repeatedly and desperately calling for help from the hillside.” Related: Trial Begins for Man Accused of Sparking Deadly Palisades Fire A federal prosecutor told jurors that Rinderknecht hiked to a spot known as Hidden Buddha Hill, above a neighborhood filled with multimillion-dollar homes stretching down to Malibu on the coast, and intentionally set a fire in the bushes with a barbecue lighter later found in his car. The blaze he’s accused of igniting, known as the Lachman Fire, was contained by firefighters, but was allegedly rekindled on Jan.

item.title: Tesla’s Supervised Full Self-Driving Rollout to Begin Soon in Denmark
item.description: Tesla on Tuesday said supervised full self-driving has been approved in Denmark, and the rollout will begin soon, marking another European country to recognize the electric vehicle maker’s autonomous driving technology. Related: Tesla’s Musk Expects Widespread US Use of Cars Without Human Monitors This Year Estonia cleared Tesla’s FSD for its roads last month, following Dutch regulators, who in April became the first in Europe to approve Tesla’s supervised self-driving software. The company has been banking on self-driving to boost vehicle sales, which have slowed amid intensifying competition, an aging lineup and CEO Elon Musk’s conservative political rhetoric that has alienated some potential customers.

item.title: Gallagher Australia poaches senior corporate broking trio from Aon
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Aviso Specialty hires Nicholas Martin to lead new risk intelligence division
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Liberty’s HK and Singapore distribution chief Jacky Chan departs
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: FCA decides to fine Carlos Ricardo Fuenmayor £99,600 for disclosure failures
item.description: The FCA has decided to fine Carlos Fuenmayor, the Chief Executive of BancTrust, £99,600 for failing to disclose 3 separate matters to the FCA. Mr Fuenmayor has referred his Decision Notice to the Upper Tribunal where he and the FCA will present their cases. Mr Fuenmayor failed, until December 2021, to tell the FCA, including in application forms that he submitted on behalf of BancTrust, that he had been placed under investigation by the US Financial Industry Regulatory Authority in December 2017 and was then sanctioned by them in June 2019.

item.title: HCI targets tokenized reinsurance capital support for Fortex Re, via Oxbridge Re’s SurancePlus
item.description: HCI Group, Inc., the insurance holding company, is tapping into the wave of interest in taking risk on-chain and issuing tokenized reinsurance securities as a way to access risk capital through a partnership with Oxbridge Re, that sees three tokens being offered to support risks held by its recently formed reinsurer Fortex Re. Now, it transpires that HCI is accessing reinsurance capital through three tranches of tokenized reinsurance securities, with Fortex Re acting as the conduit to cede risk to the capital and investors that will ultimately back the risk, working with Oxbridge Re and its subsidiary SurancePlus. Oxbridge Re and SurancePlus said they will launch three tokenized reinsurance securities that will provide investors with access to “synthetic contractual returns and catastrophe exposures based on the outcome of reinsurance contracts underwritten by HCI’s reinsurance subsidiary, Fortex Reinsurance SPC, Ltd.

item.title: Arthur Re adds another dimension to Gallagher Securities’ global retro offering: CEO Bolding
item.description: platform adds another dimension to the reinsurance broker’s global retrocession offering, and according to Jason Bolding, Global CEO of Gallagher Securities, the platform allows the firm to bring clients more flexible capital solutions while streamlining the issuance process for index-based catastrophe bonds. “I think it adds another dimension to our global retro offering, allowing us to bring clients more flexible capital solutions. Artemis recently spoke with Bolding about the Bermuda-domiciled unrestricted special purpose insurer (SPI) and segregated accounts company, which was established last year by Gallagher Re and its specialist capital markets division, Gallagher Securities.

item.title: $100bn+ loss event required to materially alter reinsurance pricing: Jefferies
item.description: Analysts at Jefferies have suggested a major loss event exceeding $100 billion would likely be required to materially alter reinsurance market conditions, following a 15–20% decline in property catastrophe pricing at the June renewals. Jefferies observed that the 2026 hurricane season comes several years after the $50 billion+ industry loss Hurricane Ian, which drove the hard reinsurance market in 2023, followed by a moderation in the subsequent three years. With this in mind, the firm underlined a ~$100 billion+ event, particularly one centred in Florida, as the threshold needed to fundamentally shift the reinsurance and CAT bond markets.

item.title: Cyber market to benefit from clear protocols governing review process for BI claims: IUA
item.description: A new report from the International Underwriting Association (IUA), a representative body for insurance and reinsurance companies operating in the London Market, has urged the establishment of clear protocols related to the review process for business interruption (BI) claims to benefit the cyber insurance market. The IUA’s new paper, ‘Split Market Reviews in Cyber BI Towers: Why It’s Time for a Rethink’, outlines how the layered structure of cyber towers currently poses several challenges in claims review. We are therefore delighted to partner with the IUA on this report, where we share some of our thoughts and observations, based on our own claims experience, of what a market protocol could include to help smooth the process for insurers and policyholders alike.” To build on the findings of the new report, the IUA has announced that it will be convening market practitioners to consider potential solutions and practical next steps for the cyber insurance market in the coming months.

item.title: Pinpoint UK Solutions gets FCA authorisation
item.description: Pinpoint UK Solutions Ltd, part of AM Specialty Insurance Group (ASIG), has received authorisation from the Financial Conduct Authority (FCA) to operate as a directly authorised company in the UK market. The authorisation follows Pinpoint UK’s Lloyd’s coverholder approval in early 2026, It’s also expected to support the development of Pinpoint UK’s business lines, including UK Enterprises, led by Joe Harding, focused on building scalable specialty insurance solutions and strengthening broker and client relationships across the UK market. This authorisation is an important milestone and aligns with ASIG’s long-term growth plan to develop Pinpoint UK, further supporting its ambition to expand its specialty insurance platform across the UK markets.

item.title: US Derivatives Regulator Maps Out Rules for Soaring Prediction Market Industry
item.description: derivatives regulator on Wednesday released new draft regulations governing the burgeoning prediction markets industry, seeking to cement federal oversight over companies that have shot to the forefront of politics and sports betting while presenting new possible avenues for fraud. Instances of alleged insider trading have multiplied in recent months, although the prediction market companies have maintained that they themselves detected and reported them: a U.S. The proposal, if adopted, would set standards for the types of sporting wagers that are acceptable while leaving prediction markets on elections and politics outside a category of activities that garner stricter review, according to a draft made public by the U.S.

item.title: People Moves: Andersen to Lead Nationwide Agribusiness Distribution; Ascot Names Eppers Group Chief Investment Officer; Baron Joins Zurich as US Head of Energy
item.description: Ascot Appoints Eppers as Group Chief Investment Officer Ascot Group Limited appointed Joseph Eppers as group chief investment officer and a member of the company’s global executive committee. Eppers has more than 25 years of industry experience and joins Ascot from Selective Insurance, where he served as executive vice president and chief investment officer, overseeing the company’s investment portfolio and strategy. Andersen named leader of Nationwide Agribusiness Distribution and Underwriting Nationwide named Nick Andersen as the new leader of agribusiness distribution and underwriting, following Dirk Pollitt’s retirement.

item.title: USDOT Rescinds ‘Disparate Impact’ Civil Rights Regulation
item.description: Transportation Department said Wednesday it is rescinding part of its long-standing civil rights regulations that prohibit conduct that has an unintended “disparate” impact. Transportation Secretary Sean Duffy said the department is making clear its regulations “prohibit only intentional discrimination, not conduct or activities that have a disparate impact,” and added USDOT will not take action on disparate-impact liability. Curbing so-called disparate impact liability, which is common in employment-related cases, removes a tool the government has used for decades to also police discrimination in housing, education, lending and other areas.

item.title: Willis launches capacity revenue protection solution for PJM energy producers
item.description: Willis, a WTW business, announced the launch of Capacity Revenue Protection, a solution designed to help PJM energy producers safeguard revenue when physical damage leads to UCAP (Accredited Unforced Capacity) reductions and prolonged capacity payment impacts. Capacity Revenue Protection is designed to provide predictable support through a parametric structure aligned with PJM’s market rules and capacity requirements, helping producers stabilize cash flow during extended recovery and recertification periods. Capacity Revenue Protection gives our clients a way to protect revenue long after the physical repairs are complete.

item.title: Broker legal battles show no sign of abating, even after landmark court ruling
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: El Niño raises winter bushfire risk across Australia: IAG
item.description: A developing El Niño climate cycle is set to heighten bushfire risk across large parts of Australia this winter, according to NRMA Insurance, the retail arm of IAG. Your company may already have a corporate license in place.

item.title: GEICO names David Foy CFO
item.description: GEICO announced that David Foy has been appointed chief financial officer, effective June 29. “David is a proven finance executive, and we are pleased to welcome him to GEICO. Based at GEICO’s headquarters in Bethesda, Foy will report to Nancy Pierce, GEICO’s chief executive officer, and oversee all finance and real estate functions.

item.title: Roblox Wants Deluge of Child Sex Abuse Cases Moved Out of Court
item.description: “They don’t want everybody to know what actually is going on.” Roblox is facing more than 150 child-safety suits consolidated in federal court, in addition to scores of others in state courts. An administrator explained that the FBI had intercepted a man in Australia, who posed as a child on Roblox, from meeting up with her daughter in person. But if Roblox gets it way, those families might never see their day in court.

item.title: CSU Adjusts Atlantic Hurricane Season Forecast Due to Emerging El Niño
item.description: The growing likelihood of an El Niño to emerge this summer has led Colorado State University hurricane researchers to slightly reduce their original forecast for the Atlantic hurricane season. Related: Weathering NOAA’s ‘Below Normal Atlantic Hurricane Season’ And A Super El Niño “When you get an El Niño, it tends to increase winds high up in the atmosphere in the Atlantic, which basically cause a lot of sheer—strong winds that tear apart hurricanes as they’re trying to develop and intensify,” said Phil Klotzbach, a senior research scientist in the Department of Atmospheric Science at CSU. Related: US Forecaster Says El Niño Has 82% Chance of Developing by July While this hurricane season is forecast to be below average, there will probably be little pockets in certain areas where conditions are conducive for a storm, Klotzbach said.

item.title: Stellantis Tells 1.3M Jeep Owners to Park Outside Over Fire Concerns
item.description: Stellantis is recalling more than 1.3 million Jeep SUVs and trucks worldwide due to fire concerns and is urging owners to park outside, away from structures or other vehicles until a fix is completed. NHTSA opened an investigation in September 2024 into nearly 800,000 Jeep Wrangler and Gladiator vehicles from the 2021-2023 model years over engine fire concerns, saying the hydraulic power steering pump wiring may overheat and cause a vehicle fire, even when the vehicle is parked with the ignition in the “Off” position. In a filing with the National Highway Traffic Safety Administration on Tuesday, Stellantis said the recall covers 2021 through the 2025 model year Jeep Wrangler SUVs and Jeep Gladiator trucks over an electrical connection issue in the electric hydraulic power steering pump wiring.

item.title: California Road Rage Incident Leads to Insurance Fraud Conviction
item.description: A California man was convicted last week of trying to profit from a road rage incident by submitting a claim to his insurance company for the damage he caused to his own car, according to an announcement by the Santa Clara County District Attorney. on ramp when he got into a road rage incident, captured on the commercial truck’s dashcam. Trying to profit from your own road rage is outrageous, and we will seek an appropriate punishment.” Progressive Insurance is seeking restitution of $1,200.

item.title: FBI Warns of Rising Cargo Theft Threat
item.description: The FBI Philadelphia Field Office issued an alert to the transportation and logistics sector about a significant increase in cargo theft scams targeting businesses nationwide. In 2025, estimated cargo theft losses in the United States and Canada reached nearly $725 million, a 60 percent increase from 2024, while confirmed cargo theft incidents increased by 18 percent, according to government data. A recent FBI Internet Crime Complaint Center (IC3) public service announcement indicated cyber-enabled strategic cargo theft surged dramatically in 2025.

item.title: The Crunchbase Tech Layoffs Tracker
item.description: Methodology This tracker includes layoffs conducted by U.S.-based companies or those with a strong U.S. We source the layoffs from media reports, our own reporting, social media posts and layoffs.fyi , a crowdsourced database of tech layoffs. We recently updated our layoffs tracker to reflect the most recent round of layoffs each company has conducted.

item.title: CSU lowers Atlantic hurricane forecast for 2026, landfall probabilities also reduced
item.description: The Colorado State University (CSU) tropical meteorology team has issued the first update to its seasonal forecast for the 2026 Atlantic hurricane season , now calling for slightly less activity in terms of named storms and hurricanes and giving reduced probabilities of landfall in key regions, citing the increased likelihood of a moderate to strong El Niño as a primary factor. We have reduced the forecast numbers from our April outlook due to increased confidence in a moderate to strong El Niño for the peak of the Atlantic hurricane season.” A moderate to strong El Nino is now anticipated by the peak of the 2026 Atlantic hurricane season, from August to October, they further explained. We currently anticipate that the moderate to strong El Niño will dominate the tropical circulation during the peak of the 2026 Atlantic hurricane season, likely driving well above-normal levels of vertical wind shear across the tropical Atlantic and Caribbean,” the forecast further states.

item.title: US property insureds seize best market conditions in years: Artex
item.description: According to Artex’s Alternative View — Spring 2026 report, insureds in the US property insurance space are “eagerly taking advantage” of the most favourable market they have experienced in several years. With ample market capacity and carriers eager to expand their property portfolios, Artex noted in its latest report that many insureds have taken advantage of the favourable conditions to renegotiate previously restrictive terms and conditions and, in some cases, secure lower deductibles. This helped offset insured losses from the January 2025 California wildfires.” Artex explained that, following years of higher-than-average rate increases, catastrophe-driven layered and shared property programs have finally experienced rate relief.

item.title: USAA names Andrew J. Pinkes to Board of Directors
item.description: Those experiences and values will serve USAA well, and we are pleased to welcome him to the Board.” Pinkes said: “It is an honour to join USAA’s Board of Directors. Pinkes to its Board of Directors, effective 29 May 2026. According to USAA, Pinkes has held a range of senior executive and board-level positions throughout his career within the property and casualty insurance industry.

item.title: US P&C insurance industry rebounds with $16.3bn underwriting gain in Q1’26: AM Best
item.description: The US property and casualty (P&C) insurance sector recorded a $16.3 billion net underwriting gain in the first quarter of 2026, following a $1 billion loss in the same period a year prior, according to a recent report by AM Best. A 10.3% increase in net investment income earned, combined with the underwriting gain, drove pre-tax operating income up 97% to $39.5 billion. AM Best noted that catastrophe losses accounted for 4.2 points on the first quarter 2026 combined ratio, down from an estimated 14.5 percentage points a year earlier.

item.title: Flesh-Eating Cattle Parasite Spreads Beyond Texas
item.description: Three more cases of the New World screwworm have been confirmed, including one outside the main cluster in Texas, demonstrating the difficulty of stopping a resurgent pest that could devastate the nation’s cattle industry, the U.S. Along with cattle and other warm-blooded livestock, scientists worry screwworms could devastate the millions of wild white-tailed deer in Texas. The screwworm is actually a fly larva that eats living flesh instead of dead material.
